compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Fix pushed to Gerrit as change 286 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/286). Adds undocumented --include-provenance flag to `show`; default view now strips managed_refs, reconciler (_read_local_ticket_full) always passes the flag. Local: lint+mypy+34 affected tests green; verified end-to-end on kooky-graft-cap. Awaiting LLM-Review + Verified votes.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Patchset 1 CI caught a real regression: test_llm_parity_show_vs_list — to_llm actually passes managed_refs through (not in OMIT_KEYS), so stripping before the llm branch broke show<->list llm parity. Fixed by scoping the strip to the default (non-llm) view only (guard 'fmt != llm'); the reconciler uses the default format + --include-provenance, and the llm view is left intact for parity. Local: lint+mypy green, 897 passed in affected suites incl. the parity test. Patchset 2 pushed; re-awaiting votes.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
`rebar show` (and its JSON output) surfaces the internal `managed_refs` provenance
projection in a way that reads as a **live `relates_to` link**, even after that link
has been removed with `unlink`. This makes a correctly-removed link look like it is
still present, and makes `unlink` look broken when it is working correctly.

## Context / root cause
`managed_refs` (`src/rebar/reducer/_managed_refs.py`) is a **strictly-monotonic**
projection of every logical reference a ticket has *ever* managed. Per its own module
docstring (lines 32-38) and `add_managed_ref` ("Never removes", lines 92-103) it is
**deliberately never pruned by UNLINK/detach** — it exists so a local link/parent
removal can be propagated to a peer system (Jira) without being resurrected by the
next inbound sync. That behavior is correct and intentional for reconciliation.

The problem is purely **presentation**: `show` renders `managed_refs` alongside the
live link data with a `relates_to <target>` shape indistinguishable from an active
relation, with nothing marking it as "removed / provenance-only." The live link set
(`deps`) is the source of truth for what links actually exist.

## Reproduction Steps
1. `rebar link A B relates_to`  (creates the link).
2. `rebar unlink A B`  (removes it; reciprocal side removed too for relates_to).
3. `rebar deps A`  → shows `deps: []` (link is genuinely gone).
4. `rebar show A`  → still shows `managed_refs: [["relates_to", "B"]]`.
5. `rebar unlink A B` again → `Error: no LINK event found …` (confirms nothing to remove).

- **Expected:** after unlink, `show` does not present B as a current `relates_to`
  relation of A. Any provenance/ever-managed info is either hidden from the default
  human view or clearly labeled as removed/provenance-only, distinct from live links.
- **Actual:** `show` presents `managed_refs` such that B looks like a current
  `relates_to` link of A, contradicting `deps: []` and misleading the reader into
  thinking `unlink` failed.

## Impact
- Users (and agents) reading `show` conclude an unlinked relation is still present,
  and that `unlink` is broken — wasted debugging (this bug was filed after exactly
  that confusion). It undermines trust in the unlink/link surface.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] After a successful `unlink`, the default human `rebar show` output does not
      present the removed relation as a current/live link.
- [ ] If provenance (`managed_refs`) is shown at all in user-facing output, it is
      clearly labeled as provenance/ever-managed (removed links distinguishable from
      active ones) — not rendered identically to live `deps` relations.
- [ ] The live link source of truth (`deps`) and the monotonic `managed_refs`
      projection are visually/structurally distinct in `show`/JSON so they can't be
      conflated.
- [ ] `managed_refs` remains monotonic (this bug must NOT be "fixed" by pruning it on
      UNLINK — that would reopen the Jira removal-resurrection window the field guards).
- [ ] A regression test asserts that `show` for a ticket with a removed relates_to
      link does not present it as an active relation.
compiled_state.title: rebar show surfaces monotonic managed_refs as a live link, making removed (unlinked) relations look present
